Model Evaluation

Evaluation principle: PLIE-PIC is a passive liquidation-impact quantile baseline, so the primary checks are quantile coverage, pinball loss against null baselines, conditional subset behavior, monotonicity of PLIE versus liquidation pressure, and leakage/output sanity. Low full-sample return IC is not by itself a failure.

Future leakage checks

passedTrue
n_checks9
n_failed0

'asof_liquidation_alignment', 'passed': True, 'severity': 'critical', 'message': 'Liquidation feature time is never after price time, and age matches the timestamps.', 'details': {'future_liq_rows': 'critical', 'message': 'Source-clock frame has one monotonic row per liquidation snapshot.', 'details': {'duplicates': 0, 'monotonic_increasing': True, 'source_rows': 40508}}, {'name': 02:50:00+00:00'}, 'validation': {'rows': 6076, 'start': '2024-12-21 03:50:00+00:00', 'end': '2025-08-31 20:50:00+00:00'}, 'test': {'rows': 6077, 'start': '2025-08-31 21:50:00+00:00', 'end': monotonicity_metrics

variable horizon_min n_deciles rho_decile_vs_plie rho_decile_vs_aligned_actual plie_shape_pass actual_relation_note
plie_abs_main_bps 20 10 1.0000 0.3455 True Actual returns include active flow/absorption; positive monotonicity is useful but not required for PLIE validity.

plie_abs_main_bps 60 10 1.0000 0.6727 True Actual returns include active flow/absorption; positive monotonicity is useful but not required for PLIE validity.
plie_accel_pos 20 10 0.3212 0.3091 False Actual returns include active flow/absorption; positive monotonicity is useful but not required for PLIE validity.
